session-mining.blurb: Mine past sessions for lessons, verify against the live repo, ship a ranked upgrade plan
session-mining.triggers: 'review the last N sessions', 'mine my sessions', 'what did we learn from these sessions', 'what should we upgrade/merge/create from past work', 'audit the sessions for lessons', 'note down skills we can extract', or any retrospective over prior runs. Locates all three session stores, signal-extracts with offset/limit discipline (no blind read), doer-summarizes via MiniMax, STORM-synthesizes, then VERIFIES against the repo to kill every lesson already shipped. Propose-only.
session-mining.modes: Six phases: locate stores → map (session_map.py) → signal-extract (mine_sessions.py, never blind-read) → doer-summarize (MiniMax) → STORM synthesize → verify-against-repo → propose-only register. On-demand companion to skillsmith's daily routine.

skillsmith.blurb: Manage, sync, upgrade, and auto-evolve Star Alliance skills
skillsmith.triggers: 'sync my skills', 'upgrade a skill', 'create a skill', 'install my skills', 'run the skill routine', 'evolve my skills', '/skillsmith'. Scripts in scripts/; procedures in references/.
skillsmith.modes: sync: reconcile repo and device by metadata.version, honoring fork+external exceptions. upgrade: bump a skill's version, regenerate the VERSIONS.md registry, run the Cowork-compliance check, add a changelog entry. create: author a new skill via the official skill-creator, then make it upgradeable. r

spec-driven-development.blurb: Build from an executable spec — spec→plan→tasks→implement, each phase gated
spec-driven-development.triggers: 'spec this out', 'spec-driven', 'write a spec first', 'plan before we build', 'turn this into a spec', 'specify the feature', 'break this into tasks', 'what's the MVP slice'
spec-driven-development.modes: specify; clarify; plan; tasks; analyze; implement

star-alliance-language.blurb: The guild's shared reading protocol for OKF-tidied repos — read the concept map, not whole files
star-alliance-language.triggers: automatic — every member orients through it when starting work in a repo. 'read the repo', 'what's in here', 'map this', 'orient me', 'where is X documented'. Universal — rides every member like weapon-utility. The consumer half of the OKF pair (okf produces, this reads).
star-alliance-language.modes: map (default) — one-shot concept map via okf_read.py (frontmatter + cross-links, grouped, index.md first); --dir/--type/--grep narrow; --links walk one concept's graph; --json feed a doer

storm-investigation.blurb: Multi-perspective STORM research: 5 personas, contradiction map, peer-reviewed briefing
storm-investigation.triggers: on 'research this topic', 'run STORM', 'STORM this', 'investigate X deeply', 'deep dive on X', 'multi-perspective research', '5 expert perspectives', 'contradiction map', 'give me a research briefing', 'red-team this idea', or research before a decision — writing, investing, negotiating, interviewing, presenting, learning. The Stanford OVAL Lab method (NAACL 2024): one prompt returns the majority view; five contrasting personas catch the blind spots.
storm-investigation.modes: Inline (default) — run the four prompts in sequence, ~5 min, one briefing; Fan-out — one doer per persona in parallel (MiniMax M3 default, Claude sub-agents/Workflow when tools or structured output needed), then synthesize. Optional source-grounding via web/MCP.

strategies-review.blurb: Reviews pending strategies then promotes to executed and verifies

supabase.blurb: All-in-one Supabase toolkit spanning database auth storage vectors
supabase.triggers: Supabase products (Database, Auth, Edge Functions, Realtime, Storage, Vectors, Cron, Queues); client libraries and SSR integrations (supabase-js, @supabase/ssr) in Next.js, React, SvelteKit, Astro, Remix; auth issues (login, logout, sessions, JWT, cookies, get

supabase-postgres-best-practices.blurb: Postgres performance optimization and best practices from Supabase

system-prompt-design-patterns.blurb: Design & harden production system prompts — identity, capabilities, tool-use, refusal, persona, anti-jailbreak (8 vendors)
system-prompt-design-patterns.triggers: 'design a system prompt', 'review this system prompt', 'harden this agent prompt', 'how do production prompts handle X'
system-prompt-design-patterns.modes: structural patterns; tool-use & refusal; persona & anti-jailbreak; cross-vendor comparison

timeseries-forecasting.blurb: Forecast a numeric series with Google TimesFM — zero-shot point + quantile intervals, covariates, rolling backtests
timeseries-forecasting.triggers: 'forecast this series', 'predict the next N periods', 'project this metric forward', 'forecast demand'
timeseries-forecasting.modes: foundation vs classical; API & usage; evaluation & pitfalls; quant application

trading-strategy.blurb: Read-only trading-strategy design that ships a written, dated strategy spec and never places a
trading-strategy.triggers: 'build a trading strategy', 'design an entry/exit plan', 'backtest this idea', 'how should I size this trade', 'turn this view into a strategy'

transactions-domain-model.blurb: Loads Lex Council transactions domain model before any work
transactions-domain-model.triggers: , transaction templates, or any transaction-related bug, feature, or refactor. Also trigger when the user says "work on transactions", "fix a transaction bug", "add a transaction feature", "transaction page", or references any file under finances/. Load silent

ultra-brainstorming.blurb: The Strategist's super-planning: fuse members' outputs into one plan across many models
ultra-brainstorming.triggers: 'ultra-brainstorm this', 'super-plan this', 'synthesize the members' work', 'brainstorm this across models', 'merge these proposals into one plan', 'plan this deeply before we build', or any fan-in where several specialists feed one build. A MODEL ensemble (several thinking models), distinct from storm-investigation's persona ensemble.

ux-copywriting.blurb: Functional in-product copy — microcopy, error/empty/loading/success states, onboarding, confirmations; one voice, plain-language, accessible
ux-copywriting.triggers: 'write the error message', 'microcopy for this button', 'empty-state copy', 'onboarding copy', 'reword this confirmation'
ux-copywriting.modes: state-copy patterns; voice & concision; accessible copy

ux-research.blurb: UX research — pick a method (interviews/usability/surveys/card sort), write unbiased scripts, run studies, synthesize into personas/journeys/findings
ux-research.triggers: 'run user research', 'plan a usability test', 'write interview questions', 'map the user journey', 'synthesize this research', 'build a persona'
ux-research.modes: choosing a method; running studies; synthesis & artifacts

vault-log-compliance.blurb: Enforces P8 vault-logging compliance across all backend code
vault-log-compliance.triggers: , RPC functions, RLS policies, edge functions, cron jobs), frontend code (components, pages, hooks, mutations, stores), schema changes, or bug fixes — even if the user doesn't explicitly ask for a vault log. Also use when the user says phrases like "vault log

view-registry.blurb: Type every Supabase view name through VIEWS-dot-key — refactor-safe, append-only columns, one view per page.
view-registry.triggers: 'add a new view', 'rename a view', 'find which page reads this view', 'avoid view drift', 'wire a page to a view', 'list our views'
view-registry.modes: typed VIEWS constant; append-only columns; one view per page; registry + migration + consumer in one commit

voices-check.blurb: Integrates an agent's competing sub-voices into one coherent answer instead of letting one dominate.
voices-check.triggers: 'name the voices', 'which voice is leading', 'I am torn between', 'this feels one-sided', 'integrate the perspectives', 'am I over-indexing'

volume-price-analysis.blurb: Anna Coulling's VPA: read volume confirming/contradicting price, the insider cycle, VAP
volume-price-analysis.triggers: 'read this chart with volume', 'is this accumulation or distribution', 'why is volume contradicting price', 'what is a selling climax', 'explain volume price analysis'
volume-price-analysis.modes: effort vs result; insider cycle (accumulation/distribution/climax); VPA candle signals; volume at price

weapon-utility.blurb: Each member's rule for drawing a weapon: thinkers plan & review, doers execute
weapon-utility.triggers: automatic — every member consults it before drawing a weapon. 'which weapon', 'which model should X use', 'pick the weapon', 'thinker or doer', 'draw a weapon', 'run the weapon loop', 'how does the member choose its model'. The atomic layer beneath members-formation (which member) and ultra-brainstorming (fuse across models).
weapon-utility.modes: solo (default) — one weapon at a time: the highest-priority available thinker plans and prompts, the highest-priority available doer executes and returns, the thinker reviews against the plan and re-prompts until it conforms; ultra — when ultra-brainstorming is active, all available thinker weapons fire at once and the top thinker consolidates them into one plan before the doer runs

workflow-forge.blurb: Distil a finished run into a reusable star-map workflow
workflow-forge.triggers: 'save this as a workflow', 'add this to the star map', 'register a workflow', 'crystallize this formation'

workflow-runner.blurb: Operate the guild's own machinery — RUN a workflows.json workflow via guild/run.py; invoke the frame/plan/efficiency/leveling primitives
workflow-runner.triggers: 'run the X workflow', 'execute this workflow', 'dry-run the workflow', 'frame this brief', 'show the efficiency report', 'show the promotion queue'
workflow-runner.modes: running a workflow; the script primitives; run outputs & gates
